name: AIR T INC
summary: Air T, Inc. is a Delaware-incorporated holding company operating in four segments: Overnight Air Cargo (MAC, CSA, WASI providing FedEx feeder cargo services); Ground Support Equipment (GGS manufacturing aircraft deicers, lifts, tow tractors); Commercial Aircraft, Engines and Parts (Contrail, AirCo, Worthington, Jet Yard managing/leasing aviation assets, parts sales, disassembly); and Digital Solutions (Delphax, Shanwick, WACD providing digital aviation subscription services). FedEx contracts represent approximately 39% of consolidated revenue.

products.name: Aircraft Deicers
products.description: Mobile deicing equipment for airlines, airports, military and industrial customers manufactured by GGS in Olathe, Kansas

products.name: Scissor-type Lifts
products.description: Specialized ground support equipment manufactured by GGS

products.name: Flight-line Tow Tractors
products.description: Specialized ground support equipment for airline flight-line operations

products.name: Glycol Recovery Vehicles
products.description: Specialized vehicles for glycol recovery at airports

products.name: Decontamination Units
products.description: Military and civilian decontamination units manufactured by GGS

products.name: Digital Aviation Services
products.description: Recurring subscription-based digital aviation and business services provided to aviation industry customers

products.name: Aircraft MRO Services
products.description: FAA/EASA Part 145 repair station services including inspections, structural repairs, engine service and avionics provided by WASI

products.name: Commercial Aircraft Parts
products.description: Surplus and aftermarket commercial jet engine components, parts sales, disassembly/part-out and overhaul services

partnerships.description: Dry-lease agreements with MAC and CSA spanning over 40 years for feeder cargo operations across eastern US, upper Midwest and Caribbean; expires August 31, 2026
MAC operates Cessna Caravan 208B and SkyCourier 408 aircraft; MAC operated first commercial revenue-service flight for Cessna 408 SkyCourier in 2023
